free_text: TIAL ELASTIC AND INELASTIC NEUTRON CROSS SECTIONS ON
THE 150 MEV GEEL ELECTRON LINAC, GELINA, GIVING
AN AVERAGE BEAM CURRENT OF 50 MICROAMPS. THE REPETI-
TION RATE WAS 800 HZ AND THE BURST WIDTH 1 NS.
.WHITE NEUTRON SOURCE, WITH AN AVERAGE NEUTRON RATE OF
2.0E+13 PER SECOND. THE NEUTRON BEAM WAS COLLIMATED TO
50 MM DIAMETER, AND TRANSPORTED IN AN EVACUATED BEAM
A SYSTEM OF 8 NE213 DETECTORS, 2 INCH THICK BY
2 INCH DIAMETER, POSITIONED AT THE FOLLOWING AVERAGE
ANGLES WITH REFERENCE TO THE BEAM DIRECTION
THE DISTANCE BETWEEN THE SAMPLE CENTER AND THE DETEC-
TOR FRONT WAS 20 CM. PULSE-SHAPE DISCRIMINATION TECH-
NIQUES WAS USED TO REMOVE GAMMA RAYS. THE DETCTOR
EFFICINCY WAS CALCULATED USING THE MONTE CARLO CODE
GRESP FROM PTB IN BRAUNSCHWEIG, CONSIDERING EXPERIMEN-
A STANDARD 235-U FISSION CHAMBER AT A DISTANCE
OF 27.545 M FLIGTH PATH WAS USED AS FLUX MONITOR.
WITH DATA FROM SCHRACK ET AL.
.THE DATA WERE CORRECTED FOR SAMPLE-OUT AND FOR THE
GAMMA BACKGROUND INDUCED BY SCATTERED NEUTRONS IN THE
BEAM TUBE AND DETECTOR HOUSINGS. THE DATA WERE FURTHER
CORRECTED FOR PRIMARY AND SECONDARY FLUX ATTENUATION,
DEAD-TIME, MULTIPLE EVENTS AND MULTIPLE SCATTERING.
SYSTEMATIC UNCERTAINTY. THE VALUES GIVEN ARE
SAMPLE KEYWORDS TRANSFERRED TO PROPER SUBENTRIES  + +
.DISC SAMPLE, METALLIC NATRIUM CANNED IN 0.2 MM AL,
